messages.content: You are a senior HVAC controls engineer extracting official ASHRAE Guideline 36 knowledge from a focused chapter/section. Use the provided standard context pack for definitions, cross-references, and shared control philosophy. The context pack is CONTEXT ONLY: do not extract candidates from it and do not use it as the evidence source. Extract candidates only from FOCUS SECTION TEXT, and every evidence_quote must be copied from FOCUS SECTION TEXT. Treat chunks only as evidence anchors, not extraction boundaries. FOCUS SCOPE: Extract ONLY knowledge whose primary section_id equals one of these sections or starts with one of these section prefixes followed by a dot: 5.8. Use the rest of the manual only as context for definitions, cross-references, and terminology. Do not return candidates from outside the focus scope. Evidence quotes must come from the focus scope text, not from unrelated sections used only as context. Allowed knowledge_type values: operational_sequence, parameter_spec, fault_diagnostic_rule, commissioning_step, application_guidance. Prefer high-value operational knowledge: control sequences, enable/disable logic, reset logic, staging logic, setpoints, timers, alarm/fault diagnostic rules, commissioning/TAB checks, and application rules. Do not extract front matter, legal text, table-of-contents lines, bibliography, generic definitions without operational use, or unsupported paraphrases. If the same knowledge appears multiple times, return it once with the clearest authoritative evidence_quote. evidence_quote MUST be a verbatim contiguous substring copied from the manual and it MUST support every important fact in summary, trigger_condition, required_behavior, configurable_values, fault_condition, or commissioning_check. For operational_sequence, quote the complete rule sentence or table row that includes the condition and commanded behavior. For fault_diagnostic_rule, quote the alarm or fault condition itself. For commissioning_step, quote the check or verification action. A section heading alone, a half sentence, an editor instruction such as retain/delete this section, or an introductory phrase is NOT sufficient evidence. Do not use ellipses. Do not combine non-contiguous bullets. If one exact quote cannot support the candidate, make the candidate narrower or skip it. If a requirement has alternatives, split them into separate candidates unless one exact quote supports the combined item. Return at most 40 candidates. Prefer fewer high-confidence candidates with complete evidence over broad coverage with weak evidence. Keep evidence_quote concise, usually one complete sentence or one complete table row, not a paragraph. Preserve section_id and page_hint from nearby headings and [[page=...]] anchors. Return strict JSON with fields candidates and skipped_reason only.

We are asked to extract knowledge from section 5.8 of ASHRAE Guideline 36-2021. The focus sections are exactly "5.8". We need to extract candidates with knowledge_type: operational_sequence, parameter_spec, fault_diagnostic_rule, commissioning_step, application_guidance. Evidence quotes must be from the focus section text (pages 83-87). We must not use context pack as evidence, only the focus section. The context pack is for definitions and cross-references.

From the focus section text, we see the following subsections: 5.8.1 to 5.8.8. I'll go through and extract candidates.

First, note that section 5.8.1, 5.8.2, 5.8.3 refer to other sections; they are not operational rules themselves, but they might be parameter_spec if they specify setpoints. However, they refer to sections for definitions, so they are not directly extractable as knowledge from this section. So I'll skip those.

5.8.3.1 defines Pfan-z: "Pfan-z is the lowest rate at which the fan will operate when it is turned on but has the lowest possible speed signal from the BAS." This is a parameter definition. knowledge_type: parameter_spec.

5.8.4 and Table 5.8.4: Active endpoints for control logic depending on Zone Group mode. This is an operational specification. Table 5.8.4 shows that for different modes (Occupied, Cooldown, Setup, Warmup, Setback, Unoccupied), the Cooling maximum endpoint is Vcool-max (except Warmup, Setback, Unoccupied where it's 0) and Minimum endpoint is Vmin* (but 0 in modes other than Occupied). This is parameter_spec or operational_sequence? It defines endpoints used in control. It's essentially a parameter spec: endpoints as function of mode.

5.8.5: Control logic description. Includes notes. The actual sequences are in 5.8.5.1 to 5.8.5.5.

5.8.5.1: When Zone State Is Cooling
- a: cooling loop output mapped to active airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint.
  - a.1: If supply air temperature from air handler > room temperature, active primary airflow setpoint shall be no higher than minimum endpoint.
- b: Heating coil is OFF.
- c: If ventilation according to ASHRAE 62.1-2016, in Occupied Mode only, parallel fan starts when primary airflow drops below Voz minus one half of Pfan-z and shuts off when primary airflow rises above Voz. Fan airflow rate setpoint = Voz minus current primary airflow setpoint.
- d: If ventilation according to California Title 24, in Occupied Mode only, parallel fan starts when primary airflow drops below Zone-Abs-OA-min minus one half of Pfan-z and shuts off when primary airflow rises above Zone-Abs-OA-min. Fan airflow rate setpoint = Zone-Abs-OA-min minus current primary airflow setpoint.
- Note about designer ensuring sum of indirect ventilation meets Standard 62.1.

These are operational sequences. Could extract multiple candidates.

5.8.5.2: When Zone State Is Deadband
- a: active primary airflow setpoint shall be minimum endpoint.
- b: Heating coil is OFF.
- c: If ASHRAE 62.1, parallel fan runs if active primary airflow setpoint is below Voz. Fan airflow rate setpoint = Voz minus active primary airflow setpoint.
- d: If Title 24, in Occupied Mode only, parallel fan runs if active primary airflow setpoint is below Zone-Abs-OA-min. Fan airflow rate setpoint = Zone-Abs-OA-min minus active primary airflow setpoint.
- Note: designer must ensure sum meets Standard 62.1.

5.8.5.3: When Zone State is Heating
- For systems with electric reheat, ensure minimum airflow provided by parallel fan at minimum speed exceeds minimum required airflow for electric heater.
- a: active primary airflow setpoint shall be minimum endpoint.
- b: Parallel fan shall run.
- c: From 0% to 50%, Heating Loop output shall reset discharge temperature from current AHU SAT setpoint to a maximum of MaxΔT above space temperature setpoint.
  - Note about Standard 90.1 limit of 11°C (20°F).
- d: From 50% to 100%, Heating Loop output shall reset parallel fan airflow setpoint from the airflow setpoint required in deadband (see above; this is Pfan-z if deadband setpoint is less than Pfan-z) proportionally up to the maximum heating-fan airflow setpoint (Pfan-htgmax).

5.8.5.4: heating coil modulated to maintain discharge temperature at setpoint. (Directly controlling heating off zone temperature control loop is not acceptable).

5.8.5.5: VAV damper modulated to maintain measured primary airflow at primary airflow setpoint.

These are all operational sequences.

5.8.6: Alarms - fault_diagnostic_rule.

5.8.6.1 Low Primary Airflow:
a: If measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > zero, Level 4 alarm.
b: If < 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > zero, Level 3 alarm.
c: If zone has Importance-Multiplier of 0 for static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed.

5.8.6.2 Low-Discharge Air Temperature:
a: If heating hot-water plant is proven ON, and DAT is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, Level 4 alarm.
b: If 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, Level 3 alarm.
c: If Importance-Multiplier of 0 for hot-water reset T&R control loop, low-DAT alarms suppressed.

5.8.6.3 Fan alarm: status input different from output command after 15 seconds after change in output status.
a: Commanded ON, status OFF Level 2
b: Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4

5.8.6.4 Airflow Sensor Calibration: If fan serving zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading above larger of 10% of cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, Level 3 alarm.

5.8.6.5 Leaking Damper: If damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading above larger of 10% of cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while fan serving zone is proven ON, Level 4 alarm. Note about constant value thresholds.

5.8.6.6 Leaking Valve: If valve position is 0% for 15 minutes, and DAT is above AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), Level 4 alarm.

All these are fault_diagnostic_rule.

5.8.7: Testing/Commissioning Overrides - commissioning_step. It says: Provide software switches that interlock to a system level point to
a. force zone airflow setpoint to zero,
b. force zone airflow setpoint to Vcool-max,
c. force zone airflow setpoint to Vmin,
d. force damper full closed/open,
e. force heating to off/closed,
f. turn fan on/off, and
g. reset request-hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the next section).

These are commissioning steps: provide override capabilities.

5.8.8: System Requests - operational_sequence or parameter_spec? They define request generation logic for cooling SAT reset, static pressure reset, hot-water reset, and heating-hot water plant requests. These are operational_sequence.

5.8.8.1 Cooling SAT Reset Requests:
a. If zone temperature exceeds zone's cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests.
b. Else if ... by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes..., send 2 requests.
c. Else if Cooling Loop > 95%, send 1 request until Cooling Loop < 85%.
d. Else if Cooling Loop < 95%, send 0 requests.

5.8.8.2 Static Pressure Reset Requests:
a. If measured airflow < 50% of setpoint while setpoint > zero and damper position > 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests.
b. Else if < 70% ... and damper position > 95% for 1 minute, send 2 requests.
c. Else if damper position > 95%, send 1 request until damper position < 85%.
d. Else if damper position < 95%, send 0 requests.

5.8.8.3 If There Is a Hot-Water Coil, Hot-Water Reset Requests:
a. If DAT is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests.
b. Else if DAT is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 2 requests.
c. Else if HW valve position > 95%, send 1 request until HW valve position < 85%.
d. Else if HW valve position < 95%, send 0 requests.

5.8.8.4 If There Is a Hot-Water Coil and a Heating Hot-Water Plant, Heating-Hot Water Plant Requests:
a. If HW valve position > 95%, send 1 request until HW valve position < 10%.
b. Else if HW valve position < 95%, send 0 requests.
